<!-- Generated by purlin:spec-from-code. Review and refine. -->
# Anchor: schema_spec_format

> Scope: specs/**/*.md

## What it does

Defines the canonical 3-section format for all Purlin feature specs. Every spec file must contain `## What it does`, `## Rules`, and `## Proof` sections. Rules use `RULE-N:` numbering; proofs use `PROOF-N (RULE-N):` numbering. Optional metadata lines (`> Requires:`, `> Scope:`, `> Stack:`) appear before the first section.

## Rules

- RULE-1: Every spec has a `## What it does` section with at least one sentence
- RULE-2: Every spec has a `## Rules` section with at least one `RULE-N:` line
- RULE-3: Every spec has a `## Proof` section with at least one `PROOF-N (RULE-N):` line
- RULE-4: Rule IDs are sequential (`RULE-1`, `RULE-2`, ...) with no gaps or duplicates within a spec
- RULE-5: Each PROOF-N references a valid RULE-N that exists in the same spec's `## Rules` section
- RULE-6: Lines under `## Rules` that do not match `RULE-N:` format trigger a WARNING in sync_status

## Proof

- PROOF-1 (RULE-1): Parse a valid spec; confirm `## What it does` heading exists and body is non-empty
- PROOF-2 (RULE-2): Parse a valid spec; confirm `## Rules` heading exists and at least one `RULE-\d+:` match
- PROOF-3 (RULE-3): Parse a valid spec; confirm `## Proof` heading exists and at least one `PROOF-\d+ \(RULE-\d+\):` match
- PROOF-4 (RULE-4): Parse rules section; verify IDs are sequential integers starting at 1 with no gaps
- PROOF-5 (RULE-5): For each PROOF line, extract the referenced RULE-N and verify it exists in `## Rules`
- PROOF-6 (RULE-6): Insert an unnumbered line under `## Rules` and verify sync_status reports a WARNING
